title: Job Search Operating System, Experiments, and Sustainable Throughput · Claim Audit: Failure Recovery
summary: A candidate relies on browser tabs and memory, duplicates work, misses follow-ups, exposes sensitive notes, and changes tactics after every outcome. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the failure recovery increment in privacy-preserving search decision system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths.
Change one bounded tactic, define an observable intermediate signal, and review enough comparable cases before inference.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Failure Recovery · Theory Failure Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A search lives in tabs and memory, duplicates applications, loses artifact revisions, stores excessive contact notes, and reacts to each rejection as a trend. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Failure Recovery contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Failure Recovery · Theory Failure Recovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths. For Predict Failure Recovery · Theory Failure Recovery, keep Failure Recovery connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A search lives in tabs and memory, duplicates applications, loses artifact revisions, stores excessive contact notes, and reacts to each rejection as a trend. The theory variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Failure Recovery condition decided your prediction.

For Predict Failure Recovery · Theory Failure Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Search systems need no recovery because the data is temporary.

options.text: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
Search systems need no recovery because the data is temporary. That claim fails the predict failure recovery evidence boundary for Failure Recovery.

For predict failure recovery, apply Failure Recovery without its stated preconditions, omit the privacy-preserving search decision system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Failure Recovery for privacy-preserving search decision system.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A search lives in tabs and memory, duplicates applications, loses artifact revisions, stores excessive contact notes, and reacts to each rejection as a trend. The worked example variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths. A defensible solution applies Failure Recovery, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to privacy-preserving search decision system.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-failure-recovery — Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-5
focus: career-failure-recovery
decision-evidence: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Search systems need no recovery because the data is temporary. This fails the Failure Recovery contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: NACE 2025 Career Readiness Quick Poll (Published 2025-09-30; reviewed 2026-07-15) — Provides bounded contemporary context for skills-based graduate hiring and institutional competency integration without turning a survey into a universal employer claim.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths.
